div.h1: Intel x86-32: User Mode Entry via IRET

p.em: Mechanism for Task Switching and User-space Initialization

ul.li: Atomicity: The IRET instruction is atomic. No interrupt can occur during the register swap.
Privilege Validation: The CPU verifies that the CS selector on the stack has a higher numeric value (lower privilege) than the current CPL.
Stack Switching: Because the privilege level changes from 0 to 3, the CPU must switch from the Kernel Stack (SS0:ESP0) to the User Stack specified in the frame.
TSS Role: Although not shown here, the TSS (Task State Segment) must contain a valid ESP0 and SS0 so that the CPU can return to the kernel on the next interrupt/syscall.

g.text: 5. IF CPL Change: POP ESP, POP SS
